title: Chehni Kothi
tagline: Chehni Kothi — spiritual town of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Chehni Kothi is a traditional Kath Kuni-style fortified tower with a temple inside, located in Chehni village, Banjar, Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Chehni Kothi is a traditional Kath Kuni-style fortified tower with a temple inside, located in Chehni village, Banjar, Himachal Pradesh, India. The 350-year-old 12-storey-high structure is made in the "Kath Kuni" method (Kath meaning "wood", and Kuni, "corner"), using only stones and wood without cement or nails. It has a temple inside, but was originally built as a fort. The tower’s age is uncertain, but it is generally regarded as a several-century-old structure (often dated to the 17th century) and is one of the largest surviving wooden towers of its kind in the Western Himalayas.

topPlaces.name: Banjar, India
topPlaces.description: Banjar (pronounced: banjāra) is a town in Kullu district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. Banjar is one of the five sub-divisions of Kullu district.

topPlaces.name: Renuka Ji
topPlaces.description: Sri Renukaji (also spelled Renuka Ji) is a Hindu shrine located on the shore of Renuka Lake in the Sirmaur district of Himachal Pradesh, India. The temple complex and adjacent wetland form a combined cultural, religious and ecological site centred on the veneration of the goddess Renuka, mother of the epic figure Parashurama.

items.note: Banjar (pronounced: banjāra) is a town in Kullu district in the state of Himachal Pradesh, India. Banjar is one of the five sub-divisions of Kullu district.

Sri Renukaji (also spelled Renuka Ji) is a Hindu shrine located on the shore of Renuka Lake in the Sirmaur district of Himachal Pradesh, India. The temple complex and adjacent wetland form a combined cultural, religious and ecological site centred on the veneration of the goddess Renuka, mother of the epic figure Parashurama.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chehni Kothi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chehni Kothi is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chehni Kothi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kullu-Manali Airport (Bhuntar) (34 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (117 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chehni Kothi?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chehni Kothi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chehni Kothi?
faq.a: Stays in Chehni Kothi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chehni Kothi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Banjar, India, Renuka Ji.
